name: Rose Petal & Lavender Angel Food Cake
recipeIngredient: One 10-inch Angel Food Cake Pan OR
12 large egg whites, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: Individual angel food cake pans are available by mail order from New York Cake & Baking Dist.; (212) 675-CAKE (and other sources).
1. Place a rack in the center of the oven and preheat to 350°F.
2. Sift the flour, confectioner's sugar, and salt together. Set aside.
3. Chop the rose petals and set aside.
4. In a dry, grease-free, 4 1/2-quart bowl of a heavy-duty electric mixer, using the whisk attachment, beat the egg whites on medium-high speed until they become frothy. Add the cream of tartar and beat until soft peaks form.
5. Gradually add the granulated sugar and beat until stiff peaks form. Add the vanilla in the final moments of beating.
6. Put the dry ingredients back in the sifter and sift over the beaten egg whites, in four additions, folding with a large rubber spatula to incorporate after each addition. Folding should be done quickly but gently in order to maintain the maximum volume of air in the egg whites.
7. Fold in the chopped rose petals and dried lavender in one addition.
8. Pour the cake batter into a 10-inch angel food cake pan, or divide the batter among the nine individual angel food cake pans.
9. Bake for 30 to 40 minutes for the 10-inch pan, or 15 to 20 minutes for the individual pans. Do not open the oven door for the first 30 minutes if using a 10-inch pan, or for the first 15 minutes if using individual pans. If the door is opened, the cake will fall without hesitation.
10. Remove the cakes from the oven and place them upside-down on a wire rack to cool completely.
11. Before removing the cakes from the pans, run a knife between the cake and the pan side.
Make the Santa Rosa plum sauce:
1. Wash the plums and remove the pits. Place the plums in the bowl of the food processor fitted with the metal chopping blade. Process the plums for 2 minutes or until they are completely puréed.
2. Force the purée through a fine-mesh sieve, using a rubber spatula, into a medium-sized bowl.
3. Using a wooden spoon, blend in the juice and zest of the orange and the vanilla extract. Add sugar to taste. The amount of sugar will vary according to the sweetness of the plums from 1 to 5 tablespoons.
Assembly:
1. Dust the cakes with confectioner's sugar.
2. Coat each dessert plate with 1/4 cup of the plum sauce and place a slice or an individual cake on top of the sauce.
3. Serve with sorbet.
